title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · Lifetime Workshop
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working object lifetime and address ledger while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of object lifetime and address ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Object Value Identity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 9394. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Object Value Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime. hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Const Volatile Limits, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Const Volatile Limits precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Const Volatile Limits, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 4428. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Const Volatile Limits, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: const owns a value and volatile makes concurrent access race-free.

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible Const Volatile Limits output and hide the violated object lifetime and address ledger assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Const Volatile Limits.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Const Volatile Limits case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe. This does not satisfy Const Volatile Limits for object lifetime and address ledger.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Const Volatile Limits condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Const Volatile Limits
steps.instruction:

Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Const Volatile Limits.
steps.why: Retrieving Const Volatile Limits through the concrete object lifetime and address ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Const Volatile Limits, keep Const Volatile Limits connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.
